description: QuantMail - Wire compose, thread, and inbox fully through the proxy layer. The inbox page, compose page, and thread page exist but some hooks may call the frontend api-client which talks to the backend directly (bypassing Next.js API routes). Ensure all mail operations go through Next.js API routes using the proxy. Also add missing routes for draft, forward, labels batch operations.
steps: Step 1: Review apps/quantmail/src/services/api-client.ts and verify its baseUrl points to '/api' or a relative URL (not directly to localhost:3010). If it targets the backend directly, update it to go through Next.js API routes at /api/*. The constructor already uses process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3010' - change the default to '/api' so all frontend requests go through the Next.js proxy layer.

Step 2: Add missing API routes for complete email workflow: (a) apps/quantmail/src/app/api/emails/compose/route.ts - POST to create a draft/compose, (b) apps/quantmail/src/app/api/emails/[id]/forward/route.ts - POST to forward an email, (c) apps/quantmail/src/app/api/emails/batch/route.ts - POST for batch archive/delete/mark-read operations.

Step 3: Add the thread reply endpoint: apps/quantmail/src/app/api/threads/[id]/reply/route.ts - POST to reply to a thread (proxy to /threads/{id}/reply on backend). This supports inline reply from the thread view.

Step 4: Wire the EmailComposer component in apps/quantmail/src/app/compose/page.tsx - ensure the handleSend function's apiClient.composeEmail() + apiClient.sendEmail() calls work through the /api/emails route (POST) and /api/emails/[id]/send route (POST). Verify the compose page imports and uses the EmailComposer component with all props connected.

Step 5: In apps/quantmail/src/app/thread/[id]/page.tsx, add an inline reply form at the bottom of the thread. Import a simplified composer (textarea + send button) that calls the thread reply API route. Wire the handleReply function to open the reply form instead of navigating to /compose.

Step 6: Add an AI summarize button in the thread view that calls /api/emails/[id]/summarize (new route). The route proxies to the backend. Display the summary in a collapsible card above the thread messages.
Step 7: Add .env.local.example file in apps/quantmail/ with QUANTMAIL_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:3010 and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=/api.
acceptance_criteria: The QuantMail api-client defaults to '/api' base URL (proxied through Next.js)
All email operations (list, read, compose, send, archive, star, delete, forward, reply) have corresponding API routes in apps/quantmail/src/app/api/
Thread view has an inline reply form that works without navigating away
Compose page sends emails through /api/emails and /api/emails/[id]/send routes
TypeScript compiles without errors: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/quantmail
findings: All verification steps pass (typecheck, build, 246 tests, lint). The api-client request method was updated to support relative base URLs without requiring window.location.origin. The existing emails/route.ts POST already proxies to /emails/compose, so a separate /emails/compose/route.ts was not needed. Pre-commit hooks ran prettier on staged files.
